text:
# Launching a Privacy-First Research Digest

Creators keep asking how to ship a digest that respects reader privacy without losing signal. Here is the minimal approach I recommend for v1:

## MVP Thesis
Deliver a weekly markdown-only roundup that summarizes 5–7 high-signal articles, redacts sensitive URL parts, and clearly states policy decisions (e.g., honoring Content-Signal). No trackers.

## Must-Haves for v1
- Markdown-only email or page export (no images required)
- HTML-to-Markdown fallback for sources that do not provide native Markdown
- URL privacy filter: strip fragments, replace sensitive query values, and mask the rest
- Source policy awareness: if ai-input=no, we block it from input and annotate why
- Simple topic tags: privacy, AI policy, security, and web standards

## Good-to-Haves (but can wait)
- Fancy UI, dark mode, custom fonts
- Real-time ingestion pipeline (batch is fine first)
- OAuth integrations; manual upload will do for beta

## Early Metrics That Matter
- 40% open rate for the first 100 subscribers
- 60% of readers click at least one summarized source per issue
- 10 feedback replies in the first month (emails or short form)

## Operations Notes
- Curate manually for the first 50–100 readers; automation can come later
- Be transparent about what you collect (ideally nothing beyond email)
- Add a one-click feedback link under each summary

## Risks
- Overbuilding before confirming demand
- Not honoring site policies; reputation risk if ignored

If you keep it lean and respectful, you can launch in a week and learn fast.
